skill.name: to-issues
skill.description: Break a plan, spec, or PRD into independently-grabbable issues on the project issue tracker using tracer-bullet vertical slices. Use when user wants to convert a plan into issues, create implementation tickets, or break down work into issues.
skill.summary: Convert plans, specs, and PRDs into approved vertical-slice implementation issues.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: Static command execution findings at SKILL.md:10 and SKILL.md:54 are false positives caused by Markdown inline code, not executable shell or Ruby code. Static weak cryptography findings are false positives caused by ordinary prose substrings such as description and descriptive. The skill does ask the agent to read from and publish to an external issue tracker, so it should be published with a warning about tracker permissions and explicit user approval.

medium_findings.title: External Issue Tracker Read and Write Actions
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to fetch referenced issue content from an issue tracker and publish approved slices as new issues. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it can affect external project systems if tracker access is broad or user approval is unclear.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly describe reading issue tracker content and publishing new issues. The workflow also requires user approval before publishing, so this is a controlled integration risk rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Inline Code Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the referenced lines contain Markdown inline code for a slash command and a triage label. No executable script, shell interpolation, or command invocation is present in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious syntax is plain Markdown text, not Ruby source code. The file is an instruction document and contains no backtick command substitution context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keywords in Prose
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography findings are keyword collisions in natural language, including frontmatter description text and issue template wording. No cryptographic function, hash algorithm selection, encryption API, or credential handling appears in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All referenced locations are prose instructions or template labels. I did not find evidence of cryptographic code or weak algorithm configuration.

dangerous_patterns.title: External System Modification
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill directs the agent to create issues on an external issue tracker after approval. This should be limited to the intended repository or project and should use the configured triage labels only.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The issue publishing step is explicit and central to the workflow. The risk depends on tracker permissions and user confirmation rather than hidden behavior.

content.user_title: Convert Plans Into Issue Tracker Tickets
content.value_statement: Large plans are hard to assign when they are not split into clear, testable work. This skill turns plans, specs, and PRDs into approved vertical-slice issues for an engineering tracker.
actual_capabilities: Breaks plans, specs, and PRDs into narrow vertical slices.
Marks each proposed slice as HITL or AFK based on required human input.
Identifies dependencies between slices before creating tracker issues.
Asks the user to approve granularity, dependencies, and issue types.
Publishes approved issues in dependency order using a consistent issue body template.
limitations: Requires an issue tracker and triage label setup outside the skill.
Depends on the conversation or tracker content for source requirements.
Does not close or modify parent issues.
Cannot guarantee perfect estimates or ownership assignments without team context.

use_cases.title: Turn a PRD into implementation issues
use_cases.description: Create thin, testable issues from a product requirement document before sprint planning.

use_cases.title: Prepare a technical plan for parallel work
use_cases.description: Split an engineering plan into independently grabbable slices with clear blockers.

use_cases.title: Convert an existing issue into child tickets
use_cases.description: Read a parent issue and create child issues that preserve scope and dependency order.

prompt_templates.title: Break down a short plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use to-issues to turn this plan into vertical-slice issues. Ask me to approve the breakdown before publishing anything.

prompt_templates.title: Create tracker issues from a PRD
prompt_templates.prompt: Use to-issues on this PRD. Include user stories covered, blockers, and HITL or AFK status for each proposed issue.

prompt_templates.title: Decompose a parent issue
prompt_templates.prompt: Use to-issues with this parent issue reference. Read the issue content and comments, then propose child issues in dependency order.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a release workstream
prompt_templates.prompt: Use to-issues to split this release plan into independently mergeable tracer bullets. Prefer AFK slices and call out any architectural decisions that require HITL review.

output_examples.input: A plan to add team invitations, email notifications, and admin controls.
output: Proposes separate vertical slices for invitation creation, invite acceptance, notification delivery, and admin revocation.
Marks the admin permission model as HITL if the source plan lacks a clear policy.
Lists blockers so foundational invitation storage is created before notification and revocation issues.

output_examples.input: A parent issue describing a billing dashboard refresh.
output: Reads the parent issue and prepares child issues linked back to the source.
Uses the project glossary in titles and acceptance criteria.
Creates issues only after the user confirms granularity and dependency order.

best_practices: Provide the tracker setup, label vocabulary, and parent issue reference before running the workflow.
Review the proposed slice list carefully before approving issue creation.
Keep source plans specific enough to define acceptance criteria for each vertical slice.
anti_patterns: Do not use it to create broad layer-based tickets such as database only or UI only.
Do not publish issues before confirming dependencies, granularity, and HITL status.
Do not use it without limiting tracker access to the intended project.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates implementation issues that represent narrow end-to-end slices of a larger plan.

faq.question: Does it publish issues automatically?
faq.answer: It should first propose the breakdown and ask for user approval before creating tracker issues.

faq.question: What are HITL and AFK slices?
faq.answer: HITL slices need human input, such as a design review. AFK slices can be implemented without extra human decisions.

faq.question: Can it work from an existing tracker issue?
faq.answer: Yes. If given an issue reference, it reads the issue body and comments before proposing child issues.

faq.question: Does it change parent issues?
faq.answer: No. The skill explicitly says not to close or modify parent issues.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
